It also includes a source map comment at the end (`//# sourceMappingURL=library.js.map`), which is typical in transpiled or bundled JavaScript code.\n\n---\n\n### Confirmed Findings:\n- **Presence of required modules:** All require statements point to local files, and the code simply re-exports these modules.\n- **No data processing or dynamic code execution:** The code does not process user input, handle untrusted data, or execute dynamic code.\n- **Source map comment:** Standard in transpiled/minified code, not suspicious.\n- **No hardcoded secrets, backdoors, or obfuscated code:** The code appears clean and standard.\n- **No malicious sinks or data flows:** No external data is being output or processed insecurely.\n\n---\n\n### Critical Evaluation of the Reports:\n\n**1.
